name: objective-gate-unified
prompt:
Consolidate the two drifted Objective Gates (agentrail/run and agentrail/afk) into one shared policy.

In agentrail/guardrails/policies/objective.py expose:
- AcCoverage(total, covered) and CheckResult(name, passed, detail) data types. CheckResult is constructed as CheckResult(name=<str>, passed=<bool>, detail=<str>) where detail is optional; AcCoverage as AcCoverage(total=<int>, covered=<int>).
- ObjectiveVerdict — a tri-state result whose tri-state value lives on the `.state` attribute (NOT `.status`) and is one of the lowercase strings 'pass' | 'fail' | 'pending'.
- ObjectiveGate and evaluate_objective(...) — the single entry point that produces an ObjectiveVerdict. evaluate_objective takes only keyword arguments, all optional: ci_checks (the async-style list of {'name': <str>, 'state': <one of 'pass'|'fail'|'pending'>} check dicts), checks (the sync-style list of CheckResult), ac_coverage (an AcCoverage), added_lines (list[str]), deleted_files (list[str]), references (dict mapping a deleted path -> list of paths that still reference it). A caller supplies either ci_checks (async harness) or checks + ac_coverage (sync harness). ObjectiveGate.evaluate accepts the same keyword arguments and returns a guardrail Verdict whose .status is the mapped VerdictStatus.
- scan_secrets(...), deleted_files_in_use(...), fix_prompt(...) helpers.

Contract — verdict attributes: ObjectiveVerdict.state is the tri-state string. .passed and .is_green are both True only when state == 'pass' (and False otherwise). .verdict is the colour string: 'green' when state == 'pass', otherwise 'red'. .failed_reasons is a list[str] naming why a non-pass verdict failed; it is empty on pass. A failing objective check contributes its check name as an entry in failed_reasons. A committed-secret hit contributes a reason whose text contains the offending marker (e.g. the word 'secret' or 'key'). A deleted-file-still-referenced hit contributes a reason whose text contains the offending file name.

Contract — evaluation order (first-violation-wins): CI status first — a failing CI/objective check short-circuits and returns immediately, BEFORE the security scan and BEFORE any pending check is considered (so a fail wins over a simultaneously-pending check and over a secret in the same diff, and only the failing check's name appears in failed_reasons). Then committed-secret scan of added_lines, then deleted-file-still-referenced (via references), then acceptance-criteria coverage. The verdict is 'pending' ONLY when the sole incompleteness is a still-running CI check. The absence of any CI signal at all — evaluate_objective(ci_checks=[]) — is 'fail', never a silent pass, and failed_reasons then contains an entry whose lowercased text includes the phrase 'no ci checks'. A committed secret in added_lines, or a deleted file still referenced, reds an otherwise all-pass run (state == 'fail').

Keep both harnesses working through this policy:
- agentrail/afk/objective_gate.py must expose evaluate(checks, added_lines, deleted_files, references) where its `checks` argument IS the async ci_checks list ({name, state} dicts); it delegates to the unified policy and returns an ObjectiveVerdict (so result.state and result.passed follow the tri-state contract). Its module-level scan_secrets, deleted_files_in_use, and fix_prompt must be the unified functions THEMSELVES (re-exported by identity — `afk_gate.scan_secrets is unified.scan_secrets`, etc., not re-implementations).
- agentrail/run/objective_gate.py must expose evaluate(checks, ac_coverage) where `checks` is the sync-style list of CheckResult; its module-level GateResult must BE the ObjectiveVerdict type (run_gate.GateResult is ObjectiveVerdict). The run harness supplies no CI checks, so its result can only ever reach state 'pass' or 'fail' (is_green True only on 'pass'), never 'pending'; on a failing check, the failing check name appears in result.failed_reasons.
- register it in the guardrails registry so that 'objective_gate' is among the names from list_guardrails(); get_guardrail('objective_gate') returns an ObjectiveGate instance whose evaluate(...) returns a Verdict mapping the tri-state onto VerdictStatus: 'pass' -> VerdictStatus.PASS, 'fail' -> VerdictStatus.FAIL, 'pending' -> VerdictStatus.ADVISORY (a surfaced hold, not a hard FAIL).
